question_title: Will the European Court of Human Rights list at least one entry labeled “Grand Chamber Judgment” on its Delivery of Judgments page?
summary: The question asks whether the ECHR's Delivery of Judgments page will show at least one entry labeled "Grand Chamber Judgment" dated March 15 - April 30, 2026. From Key Cases data, the ECHR delivers ~4-6 Grand Chamber judgments per year, giving a base rate of ~36-49% for a 41-day remaining window. However, three high-profile instrumentalization cases (Lithuania, Latvia, Poland) are pending with hearings completed in early 2025, and an EJIL Talk article from January 2026 says the Grand Chamber is "preparing to deliver" these judgments. April has had Grand Chamber judgments in both 2024 and 2025. The Grand Chamber is actively in session with hearings and referral reviews. Monte Carlo simulation combining uncertain base rates (4-10/yr) and instrumentalization case delivery (~50% in window) yields 79.3%. Adjusting upward for April patterns gives ~82%.

factors.description: Base rate: ECHR delivers ~4-6 Grand Chamber judgments per year (from Key Cases 2024-2025 data: 3 in 2024, 5 in 2025). Over 41 remaining days, Poisson model gives P(≥1) of 36-49%.

Three instrumentalization cases (C.O.C.G. v. Lithuania, H.M.M. v. Latvia, R.A. v. Poland) pending with hearings held early 2025. EJIL Talk article (Jan 5, 2026) says Grand Chamber 'prepares to deliver' these judgments. Estimated ~50% chance at least one falls in the question window.

April has had Grand Chamber judgments in both 2024 (KlimaSeniorinnen, April 9) and 2025 (Ships Waste Oil Collector, April 1). Question window includes all of April - 2/2 recent years show GC judgments in April.
Grand Chamber is actively in session: hearings scheduled March 4, 25, April 1; referral panel reviewing 9 cases on March 23. Active session increases likelihood of concurrent judgment delivery.
No Grand Chamber judgment delivery announced in forthcoming judgments for March 17-26. GC judgments typically announced 1-2 weeks before delivery. Mild negative signal for late March.
5 days of window already passed (March 15-19) with no GC judgment. Mildly reduces remaining probability.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.82 (82% probability of YES)**

Key reasoning:

- **Base rate (moderate negative):** The ECHR delivers only ~4-6 Grand Chamber judgments per year based on Key Cases lists (3 in 2024, 5 in 2025). Over the remaining 41-day window, a Poisson model gives just 36-49% probability for at least one.

- **Pending instrumentalization cases (strong positive):** Three high-profile Grand Chamber cases on EU-Belarus border pushbacks had hearings in early 2025. An EJIL Talk article from January 2026 explicitly states the Grand Chamber is "preparing to deliver" these judgments. Estimated ~50% chance at least one falls within the window.

- **April pattern (moderate positive):** April had Grand Chamber judgments in both 2024 (KlimaSeniorinnen, April 9) and 2025 (Ships Waste Oil Collector, April 1). The question window covers all of April.

- **Monte Carlo result:** Simulation combining uncertain base rates and instrumentalization timing gives 79.3%. After adjusting for the April pattern and active GC session, final estimate is 82%.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the European Court of Human Rights list at least one entry labeled “Grand Chamber Judgment” on its Delivery of Judgments page?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42647)
[Delivery of judgments - ECHR Hearings - ECHR - ECHR / CEDH](https://www.echr.coe.int/delivery-of-judgments)
[Cases pending before the Grand Chamber - ECHR Hearings - ECHR - ECHR / CEDH](https://www.echr.coe.int/cases-pending-before-the-grand-chamber)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the ECHR’s Delivery of Judgments page at https://www.echr.coe.int/delivery-of-judgments shows at least one entry dated March 15, 2026 through April 30, 2026 inclusive that is labeled “Grand Chamber Judgment” before April 30, 2026. Otherwise, it resolves to no.
fine_print: Count only entries on the named page that are explicitly labeled "Grand Chamber Judgment." Do not count Grand Chamber hearings, chamber judgments, decisions, or press releases unless the entry itself is labeled that way. If the page is temporarily unavailable, use the directly linked case page for the same delivered judgment. Candidate Sources: the specific judgment entry linked from the Delivery of Judgments page ; ECHR homepage at https://www.echr.coe.int.
